title: knema andamanica subsp. andamanica
intro: Knema andamanica subsp. andamanica, also known as the Andaman nutmeg tree, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. It is native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is also believed to have antiseptic and anti-diarrheal properties. Herbal preparations of Knema andamanica subsp. andamanica include infusions, decoctions, and oils, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its health benefits.
